page: Microsoft Azure F8-2amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 945 GB of local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-threaded performance. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, its multi-core scaling is more limited, with stress-ng multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth remains stable, with read-only cached small block speeds at 402.64 GB/sec and large block write speeds at 33.61 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the F8-2amds_v7 lies in its high memory capacity relative to its low core count. While single-threaded execution is highly efficient, multi-threaded workloads like bzip3 decompression and static web serving show weaker relative performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this configuration is optimized for memory-heavy workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU processing. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive data processing tasks that fit within a 64.0 GB footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure F8-2amds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a substantial 32.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 945 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core performance and static web serving throughput are relatively weak. This resource profile makes the server qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications with low parallel processing demands. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-heavy workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8-2amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high single-core performance, substantial RAM-to-CPU allocation, and integrated local storage for database workloads.

64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per core memory ratio
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and low-concurrency workloads
